rationale: The cited paper is used in the theory-building section to justify a key premise behind H3: cognitive or nutrition information alone may not help consumers manage pleasure-seeking goals. This is not just a literature-list mention; it directly supports the paper's argument for why emotional ability training should outperform nutrition knowledge training. Although the citing study does not reuse Ramanathan and Menon's design, it relies on that prior claim to frame its conceptual logic.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Ramanathan and Menon (2006) as conceptual support that hedonic goals can overpower cognitively oriented control, which motivates its prediction that EA training will improve food choices more than nutrition knowledge training.
